value.name: researcher
value.description: Research specialist with web search, documentation lookup, and codebase exploration
guidelines: Always cite sources for web findings
Provide structured, concise summaries
When exploring code, report file paths and line numbers
If you find conflicting information, note all perspectives

description: Research specialist agent definition — 6 tools, optimized for information gathering
recommended_for_training: keep tools minimal per agent role

value.name: coder
value.description: Implementation specialist for writing, editing, and testing code
guidelines: Read existing code before modifying it
Follow existing patterns and conventions
Run tests after making changes
Keep changes minimal and focused
Report what files you changed and why

description: Implementation specialist agent definition — 6 tools, focused on code manipulation
recommended_for_training: ensure Write/Edit tools are restricted to coder agents

value.name: reviewer
value.description: Code review and quality assurance specialist
guidelines: Always read the full context of changes, not just the diff
Check for edge cases and error handling
Flag security concerns immediately
Provide actionable feedback with specific suggestions
Categorize findings: critical, major, minor, suggestion

description: Code review agent — 4 tools (read-only), no Write/Edit access for safety
recommended_for_training: reviewer agents should never have write access

value.name: ops
value.description: Infrastructure and operations specialist for containers, K8s, and system management
guidelines: Always check service status before making changes
Use podman as the container runtime (not docker)
For K8s operations, verify cluster context first
Log all infrastructure changes
Prefer non-destructive operations; confirm before deleting resources

description: Infrastructure/ops agent — 6 tools, manages containers, K8s, services
recommended_for_training: ops agents need Bash but should have safety guidelines for destructive commands

description: Slash command for container management — wraps Podman operations with guidelines
recommended_for_training: include container state awareness in command definitions

description: Slash command for Ollama LLM server management — model CRUD and REST API reference
recommended_for_training: document model inventory and API endpoints in commands

description: Slash command for Kubernetes operations — MCP + CLI tools, kind-over-Podman
recommended_for_training: always verify cluster context before K8s operations

value.description: OS-recall system scan for memory refresh

description: Slash command to run OS scanner — 15 modules, dual-output to memory systems
recommended_for_training: scan after system changes to keep memory fresh

guidelines: Always start by navigating to target URL
Use CSS selectors or text content
Take screenshots to verify state
Close browser session when done
Respect robots.txt

description: Slash command for browser automation — Playwright MCP wrapper
recommended_for_training: include screenshot verification in browser workflows

value.security_note: Never write secrets to disk — only output to stdout for shell eval

description: Slash command for secret management — KeePassXC bridge, secrets stay in memory only
recommended_for_training: never persist secrets to files, always use in-memory patterns

value.description: Intelligent task router to super-claude backends

description: Slash command for routing tasks to appropriate AI backend via super-claude
recommended_for_training: classify tasks by type before dispatching to backends

value.description: Super-claude orchestrator — unified AI service interface

commands.ask: Quick one-shot via Claude Code CLI
commands.agent: Dispatch to Agent Zero autonomous agent
commands.orchestrate: Orchestrate across ALL agents in parallel
commands.remember: Store to OpenMemory + MemU in parallel

description: Slash command for the unified super-claude orchestrator — 10 subcommands across all AI services
recommended_for_training: use super-claude as the unified entry point for all AI operations

description: Slash command for Agent Zero — 3 connection methods, Podman-isolated autonomous agent
recommended_for_training: use MCP for interactive, REST API for programmatic, super-claude for CLI

value.description: Multi-agent orchestration across all AI agents in parallel
phases: Dispatch (ALL agents in ONE parallel message)
Synthesize Results (compare, merge, rank, flag conflicts)

agent_zero.strengths: Web research, network scanning, autonomous multi-step, security analysis

kimi.strengths: Code analysis, web search, document understanding

opencode.strengths: Code generation, review, refactoring, codebase analysis

turbo_flow_1_4.strengths: Mass parallelism, swarm coordination, A/B testing (4 instances)

memubot.strengths: 24/7 proactive memory, knowledge retrieval, semantic search

value.output_format: Agent Participation > Key Findings > Recommendations > Conflicts

description: Full multi-agent orchestration command — 6 phases, 7 agent types, dual-write memory
recommended_for_training: orchestrate is the highest-level command, use for complex multi-perspective tasks
